records.ety: [F. brigade, fr. It. brigata troop, crew, brigade, originally, a contending troop, fr. briga trouble, quarrel. See Brigand.]

defs: A body of troops, whether cavalry, artillery, infantry, or mixed, consisting of two or more regiments, under the command of a brigadier general.
notes: ☞ Two or more brigades constitute a division, commanded by a major general; two or more divisions constitute an army corps, or corps d'arme. [U.S.]

defs: Any body of persons organized for acting or marching together under authority; as, a fire brigade.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Brigaded; p. pr. & vb. n. Brigading.]

defs: To form into a brigade, or into brigades.
